title: Giving hope to those who need it most
description: BUSINESS NEWS - In the cold winter months, a...
bodyText:
Advertorial

BUSINESS NEWS - In the cold winter months, a warm meal, cozy clothing and a safe place to rest can bring a powerful sense of hope and belonging.


> Sadly, not all George residents and animals are fortunate enough to experience this comfort. That's why Vox and Frogfoot have joined forces to make a difference, offering dignity and support to children at Kidstop and animals at the Garden Route SPCA through thoughtful donations and community upliftment.


Kidstop in Borchards - a haven for children aged 7 to 18 - provides a safe, educational and fun environment where young minds are empowered through life skills, sports, arts and culture.

On 23 August 2025, Vox and Frogfoot will donate pet blankets at the SPCA and visit the kids at Kidstop to distribute food parcels, donate a jumping castle, paint a wall and host interactive fun games.

You can help tooDrop off your donations of non-perishable food items, warm clothing and pet blankets at Vox offices, 101 York Street, as well as at Vox activations, at Garden Route Mall on 23–29 September and 21–27 October 2025, where donation boxes will be available.

Together, we can bring more love, more care and more connection to those who need it most.

From fresh connections to fresh cut flowers. At Suraya's Flowers are from left: Dylan Muller (Vox sales agent), Rayvian Cupido (Vox Fibre sales agent), Willber Fredricks (Vox Fibre sales agent) and in the flowers, Dylan Malan (GRJ Vox Fibre To the Home sales manager).

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
